name: LLM Guard
pricing_notes: Open source; LLM Guard API (hosted) available via Protect AI with paid plans

auth.notes: Self-hosted library requires no auth; Protect AI hosted API uses API key

performance.notes: Scanner latency varies by scanner type; local model scanners add 50-200ms per check

agent_readiness.notes: Input + output scanners for LLM security. Detects: prompt injection, PII leakage, toxic content, code injection, off-topic responses, hallucinations, secrets exposure. Composable scanner pipeline. Works locally or via API.

gotchas: Local model scanners require downloading models (~GB each)
False positive rate requires threshold tuning
Adds latency to every LLM call (50-300ms per scanner)
Limited to English for most scanners
best_when: Production LLM applications handling sensitive data (PII, healthcare, finance)
Agents that process user inputs and need prompt injection detection
Compliance requirements for AI output filtering
Self-hosted deployments where data cannot leave your infrastructure
avoid_when: Simple content filtering needs (lighter solutions available)
summary: LLM Guard is an open-source security toolkit for LLM applications providing input and output scanners. Detects prompt injection, PII leakage, toxic content, code injection, secrets, and hallucinations. Composable pipeline — use only the scanners you need. Best-in-class for self-hosted deployments where data sovereignty matters. Latency overhead is the main tradeoff.
